summary:
Pulmonary vein distension is a hallmark finding consistent with elevated pulmonary pressures seen in Eisenmenger Syndrome. The RV remains hypertrophied rather than returning to its original state as suggested by option B. Central arterial involvement like dilated pulmonary arteries can also indicate advanced stages where remodelling has occurred from chronic high blood pressure within these vessels.
To refine it into two concise statements focusing only on critical aspects related directly back towards options provided without extraneous details about specific pathophysiology mechanisms beyond what was asked explicitly,

Revised Summary: 
Eisenmenger patients exhibit prominent pulmonary venous congestion indicating raised pulmonary vascular resistance while their Right Ventricles remain thickened unlike recovery implied under some incorrect choices presented here. Key findings include engorged venules alongside persistent enlargement of cardiac chambers particularly affecting the right side reflecting ongoing hemodynamic stress despite potential late-stage The RV remains hypertrophied rather than returning to its original state as suggested by option B. Central arterial involvement like dilated pulmonary arteries can also indicate advanced stages where remodelling has occurred from chronic high blood pressure within these vessels.
